data.description:
Sweep ticket for code-quality wins identified during a 2026-04-25 exploratory pass over the c11 source tree. Two concrete items to start, plus a section to be filled in by an exploration pass.

## Pass 1 — concrete items

### 1. Delete rejected installer code in `CLI/c11.swift` (~2,300 lines)

Lines ~13906–16252 implement the `cmux install <tui>` integration installers (Module 4). Per CLAUDE.md, this work is **policy-rejected**: 'c11 install <tui> is rejected. Any proposal that writes to a user's persistent tool config is a non-starter… The 691-line spec exists as a historical artifact only — do not revive it.'

The implementation, however, was never deleted. It is dead code by policy, not by accident.

Scope:
- Remove the `cmux install` / `cmux uninstall` subcommand handlers and routing in `CMUXCLI`.
- Remove the `InstallExitCode`, `IntegrationInstallerConstants`, `IntegrationInstallerPlan`, `IntegrationInstallerStatus` types.
- Remove the helper enums `IntegrationInstallerHelpers`, the Claude/TOML/OpenCode shim sections.
- Verify the `Resources/bin/claude` grandfathered wrapper is untouched (CLAUDE.md calls it out as a deliberate exception with a `DO NOT GENERALIZE` header).
- Update `docs/c11mux-module-4-integration-installers-spec.md` — either delete or mark as historical with a clear 'NOT IMPLEMENTED, NOT TO BE REVIVED' banner.
- Drop any tests/fixtures that exist solely to exercise the installer paths.

Risk: zero (policy-dead code, no shipping callers). Win: `c11.swift` 17,039 → ~14,700.

### 2. Carve `ContentView.swift` enum helpers into sibling files

`Sources/ContentView.swift` is 14,131 lines. The `ContentView` struct itself runs ~1420–7328 (~5,900 lines), which is genuinely a SwiftUI god-view and risky to split. But the file also contains many self-contained helper enums and structs that are de-facto separate modules already, just colocated:

- `SidebarRemoteErrorCopySupport`, `InternalTabDragConfigurationProvider`, `SidebarResizeInteraction`, `DragOverlayRoutingPolicy` (lines 65–400)
- `CommandPaletteOverlayPromotionPolicy`, `WorkspaceMountPolicy`, `MountedWorkspacePresentationPolicy` (lines 918–1420)
- `CommandPaletteSwitcherSearchIndexer`, `CommandPaletteFuzzyMatcher`, `CommandPaletteSearchEngine` (lines 7344–8309)
- `SidebarDropPlanner`, `SidebarDragAutoScrollPlanner`, `SidebarSelection`, `SidebarMaterialOption`/`SidebarBlendModeOption`/`SidebarStateOption`/`SidebarTintDefaults`/`SidebarPresetOption` (lines 12777–13997)
- `FeedbackComposerBridge` (lines 10081–10825)

These move to sibling files (`Sources/Sidebar/`, `Sources/CommandPalette/`, `Sources/Feedback/`) with **no behavior change** — pure mechanical extraction. Each block is internally cohesive and externally referenced by name. Goal: ContentView.swift down to ~8k by removing the helpers, leaving the actual View body and its tightly-coupled neighbors.

**What NOT to touch in this pass:**
- `ContentView` struct body (load-bearing for SwiftUI diffing)
- `TabItemView` and its `Equatable` contract (CLAUDE.md flags as typing-latency-critical)
- Any helper that reads from `@EnvironmentObject` / `@ObservedObject` of the ContentView (would require care to extract)

Risk: low (mechanical, type-checker enforced). Win: ContentView.swift 14,131 → ~8,000, helpers become independently navigable and testable.

## Pass 2 — exploration findings (to be appended)

Exploration pass scheduled in same session. Will append findings as comments on this ticket once complete.

## Out of scope (for now)

- `Sources/TerminalController.swift` (17,240) — single 17k-line god-class is the worst structural smell, but every change risks the typing-latency contracts CLAUDE.md flags by name. Defer until a specific pain point forces it.
- `Sources/AppDelegate.swift` (13,374) — needs investigation pass first to know what's actually in there.
- Sweeping naming or API shape changes.
data.title: Refactor cleanup: large files, dead code, structural smells

data.body:
# Pass 2 — Exploration findings

Five-agent parallel sweep on 2026-04-25 across `Sources/`, `CLI/`, `c11Tests/`, `c11UITests/`, `tests/`, `tests_v2/`, and project root. Findings ordered by actionability: real bugs first, structural debt second, cosmetic last.

## A. Real policy violations (worth fixing standalone)

### A1. Two socket-telemetry handlers use forbidden `DispatchQueue.main.sync`

CLAUDE.md "Socket command threading policy" forbids `DispatchQueue.main.sync` in high-frequency telemetry hot paths (`report_*`, `ports_kick`, status/progress/log metadata). Two handlers violate this in their fallback (non-scoped) paths:

- **`Sources/TerminalController.swift:16704`** — `report_shell_state` fallback path wraps tab resolution + surface metadata pruning in `DispatchQueue.main.sync`. This handler fires on every shell idle↔running transition.
- **`Sources/TerminalController.swift:16904`** — `ports_kick` fallback path does the same. Fires on port-scanner kicks (frequent during agent activity).

Both handlers already have an off-main "scope" branch above the `main.sync` block, so the compliant pattern exists — the legacy fallback just needs to migrate or be deleted. Migrate parsing/resolution off-main; schedule the small mutation with `DispatchQueue.main.async` (or, since these only mutate per-tab metadata, consider whether the mutation needs main-thread at all).

Risk: low (the off-main pattern is already proven in the same file). Win: removes a real source of socket-thread starvation under main-thread contention.

### A2. Test reads `Info.plist` to assert string presence (test-quality policy violation)

**`c11Tests/WindowAndDragTests.swift:379–390`** — `testAppBundleExportsInternalDragTypes` reads the bundle's `UTExportedTypeDeclarations` and asserts that the strings `"com.stage11.c11.tabtransfer"` and `"com.cmux.sidebar-tab-reorder"` are present. CLAUDE.md test-quality policy explicitly forbids tests that "read checked-in metadata or project files… only to assert that a key, string, plist entry, or snippet exists."

Two follow-ups:
- Delete the test, or replace it with a real drag-drop e2e flow that exercises the registered types end-to-end.
- Note the second asserted UTI is **`com.cmux.sidebar-tab-reorder`** — stale residue. CLAUDE.md says it should be `com.stage11.c11.sidebar-tab-reorder` (matching the sibling `com.stage11.c11.tabtransfer`). If kept, fix the UTI rename in `Resources/Info.plist` and the call sites.

### A3. Orphaned 1 GB of agent-worktree directories

**`.claude/worktrees/agent-{a438357f,a499fbaf,a59fe2e5,aa70184d}/`** — four full source-tree copies, ~253 MB each (~1 GB total), dated 2026-04-20. They are NOT registered with `git worktree list`; these are plain directory copies the harness left behind. Cleanup is `rm -rf`, not `git worktree remove`:

```
rm -rf /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11/.claude/worktrees/agent-{a438357f,a499fbaf,a59fe2e5,aa70184d}
```

They polluted the earlier `wc -l` survey by duplicating every shipping file four times. Worth grepping `.gitignore` to confirm `.claude/worktrees/` is ignored (or should be).

## B. Structural debt — context for future tickets, not for this one

### B1. `Sources/TerminalController.swift` — 17,240 lines, one class

The headline number is misleading; the file decomposes into clear subsystems, but the class itself has no separation:

| Subsystem | Lines | Methods | Extractable? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Socket lifecycle / auth | 560–1996 | ~12 | No (security boundary) |
| V2 JSON protocol dispatch | 1996–3224 | ~9 | No (`processV2Command()` is a 512-line switch at 1998–2510) |
| V2 helpers (encoding/parsing) | 3225–3519 | ~26 | **Yes** — mechanical, low coupling |
| V2 window/workspace/pane | 3554–7912 | ~54 | Partial |
| V2 surface methods | 5266–6371 | ~27 | Partial |
| V2 browser (JS eval, dialogs) | 8181–12036 | ~96 | Maybe — clear `BrowserSocketController` candidate |
| Sidebar metadata parsing | 15715–17241 | ~51 | **Yes** — pure procedural option parsing |

Specific smells:
- **`v2MainSync()` at 3237** — the `DispatchQueue.main.sync` helper used throughout the V2 layer. Every command that needs main-actor work funnels through this. It's the foundation of the threading policy violations in A1.
- **Mega-methods**: `processV2Command()` 512 lines (1998–2510); `start()` 305 lines (879–1184); `authResponseIfNeeded()` 298 lines (1280–1578) with copy-paste between V1 and V2 auth response building (1223–1248 vs 1248–1280).
- **Auth policy scattered across four entry points**: `authResponseIfNeeded()`, `socketCommandAllowsInAppFocusMutations()`, `withSocketCommandPolicy()`, `v2FocusAllowed()` — same concern, four different surfaces.
- **`nonisolated(unsafe)` mutable state at lines 42–50, 154–171, 192–199** — protected by `NSLock` (acceptable) but the V2 handle/browser maps rely on `@MainActor` for safety with no explicit lock; cross-surface websocket frame reordering could race.

Recommendation if/when this becomes a ticket: extract V2 helpers (3225–3519) and Sidebar metadata parsing (15715–17241) first — both are mechanical wins. Leave socket lifecycle, V2 dispatch, and the threading model alone until a specific pain point forces it.

### B2. `Sources/AppDelegate.swift` — 13,374 lines, ~520 methods, no MARKs

The class itself is **9,958 lines (1996–11954)** with no `// MARK:` sections at all. ~520 methods/properties with responsibilities interspersed across windows, menus, shortcuts, lifecycle, notifications, URL handling, VSCode integration, and test instrumentation.

Clear extraction candidates (sibling files, mechanical):

| Helper | Current location | Suggested home |
|---|---|---|
| `MainWindowHostingView<Content>` | 27–56 | `Sources/MainWindow.swift` |
| `CmuxTypingTiming` (DEBUG) | 56–172 | `Sources/Instrumentation.swift` |
| `CmuxMainRunLoopStallMonitor`, `CmuxMainThreadTurnProfiler` | 172–400 | `Sources/PerformanceMonitoring.swift` |
| `FinderServicePathResolver` | 400–439 | `Sources/FileSystem.swift` |
| `TerminalDirectoryOpenTarget` | 439–664 | `Sources/TerminalLaunching.swift` |
| `VSCode*` (5 types, ~400 lines) | 664–1124 | `Sources/VSCodeIntegration.swift` |
| `WorkspaceShortcutMapper`, `BrowserZoomShortcutAction` | 1124–1738 | `Sources/ShortcutHandling.swift` |
| `CmuxCLIPathInstaller` | 1152–1732 | `Sources/CLIInstaller.swift` |
| `CommandPaletteDebugSnapshot` | 1746–1996 | `Sources/CommandPalette.swift` |
| `MenuBarExtraController`, `NotificationMenuSnapshot*`, `MenuBarBadgeLabelFormatter`, etc. | 11954–13374 | `Sources/MenuBar.swift` |

Inside the class itself, the worst offender is **`handleCustomShortcut(event:)` at lines 9444–10331 — 888 lines** dispatching 15+ unrelated shortcut categories. Strategy-pattern extraction is the obvious move but bigger than this ticket should swallow.

Other smells: `AppDelegate.shared` singleton (1997) tightly coupling the codebase; 40+ weak property pairs without documented cleanup lifecycle; `DispatchSourceTimer` properties (`sessionAutosaveTimer`, `bonsplitTabDragUITestRecorder`, `browserOmnibarRepeatTickWorkItem`) without visible `invalidate()` in `deinit` — likely fine in practice (singleton lives for the app's lifetime) but a smell for any extracted owner.

Recommendation if/when this becomes a ticket: do the top-level helper extraction pass first (mechanical, no AppDelegate body changes). Save the `handleCustomShortcut` decomposition for a separate ticket since it touches every keyboard interaction.

## C. Cosmetic / minor

- **Stale `cmux_tmp` shell variable** in `CLI/c11.swift:4958` (SSH bootstrap script). Cosmetic.
- **Two CMUX-37 TODOs** in `Sources/c11App.swift:4061` and `:4150` referencing a phase-0 architectural cleanup ("express the quad as a `WorkspaceApplyPlan`"). Tracked elsewhere — leave for that owner.
- **Stage-3 mailbox TODO** in `Sources/Mailbox/MailboxDispatcher.swift:101`. Tracked elsewhere.
- **Test fixtures hardcode `cmuxterm-hq` paths** in `WorkspaceManualUnreadTests.swift:230,240,250,267,278` and `CommandPaletteSearchEngineTests.swift`. Test data, not shipping — fine for now, but worth genericizing if these tests expand.
- **17+ test methods in `c11Tests/SocketControlPasswordStoreTests.swift:244–680`** (`CmuxCLIPathInstallerTests`, `AgentSkillsOnboardingDefaultOptInTests`, `SkillInstallerPackageVersionTests`) exist solely to exercise the rejected installer code from Pass-1 item 1. Delete alongside the installer code, not separately.

## What I'm explicitly NOT recommending

- Don't refactor `TerminalController` or the V2 dispatch layer in this ticket. They sit on the security boundary and the typing-latency-adjacent socket path. Future ticket, with its own scope.
- Don't touch `handleCustomShortcut(event:)`. Same reason — every keystroke routes through it.
- Don't sweep cmux→c11 naming in shipping code beyond what falls out of the installer deletion. Per operator memory, the mirror function `mirrorC11CmuxEnv()` and the legacy keychain service string are deliberate compat aliases, and the shipped `cmux` CLI alias is policy. Whack-a-mole renaming risks breaking the compat surface for a cosmetic gain.

## Suggested splitting

If C11-14 starts feeling too big, split into:
- **C11-14a** — Delete installer code (Pass-1 item 1) + delete its tests (Section C).
- **C11-14b** — Carve `ContentView.swift` helpers into sibling files (Pass-1 item 2).
- **C11-14c** — Fix the two socket-telemetry `main.sync` violations (Section A1).
- **C11-14d** — Delete or rewrite the Info.plist string-assertion test + rename the stale `com.cmux.sidebar-tab-reorder` UTI (Section A2).
- **C11-14e** — `rm -rf` the orphan agent worktrees (Section A3) — could be its own one-line PR or just done alongside any other change.

A through E are independent and can land in any order. B-tier (TerminalController, AppDelegate, `handleCustomShortcut`) deserve their own tickets when an owner picks them up.
Reconciliation: PR #136 merged into main; deliverable shipped.
